qas.q: Bloodborne diseases are most commonly spread through mother-to-child transmission, unprotected sex, and:
Which of the following describes proper use of PPE to safeguard against exposure to bloodborne pathogens?

If you are exposed to a bloodborne pathogen while at work, your employer must offer you post-exposure evaluation and follow-up. Which of the following statements correctly describes what will happen during this post-exposure follow-up?
qas.a: Your employer will identify and document the source patient if allowed by law.

qas.q: Bloodborne diseases can be spread in the healthcare setting. This is most likely to happen if a healthcare worker:
Which of the following is an engineering control used to safeguard against exposure to bloodborne pathogens?
Which of the following is a work practice control used to safeguard against exposure to bloodborne pathogens?
qas.a: Use of alcohol rubs for routine hand hygiene

qas.q: Which of the following is a VIOLATION of a work practice control used to safeguard against exposure to bloodborne pathogens?
qas.a: A healthcare worker applies makeup in a lab where blood samples are tested.

qas.q: If you are exposed to a patient's blood, it is important to follow the acronym:
If you are exposed to a patient's blood, you should IMMEDIATELY:
qas.a: Wash the exposed area with soap and water

qas.q: Which of the following is an important bloodborne pathogen?
Which of the following is an important bloodborne pathogen?
Which of the following best describes a safeguard against exposure to bloodborne pathogens in the healthcare setting?
qas.a: Use Standard Precautions in the care of all patients.

qas.q: Listed below are some of the tasks of a healthcare worker. Choose the task that puts the worker at greatest risk for exposure to a bloodborne disease.
